title: What's Now? — year-end 2021 reflection (be ashamed to die quote)
html:
TL;DR

  • December 27, 2021 year-end note titled "What's Now?" — opens with the borrowed Horace Mann maxim "Be ashamed to die, until you have scored some victory for humanity" then drops into pragmatic todos: 2022 outline, FAFSA, fix glasses at Walmart, call chiropractor, Emilio question review, call ayoli for physical, eat with David.
  • The "What's Now?" title is the same year-end self-interrogation genre as the January 2020 "what are you searching for" line — open-ended question stapled to the top of a chore list.
  • The Horace Mann quote sits as a legacy question anchor: 19-year-old Yahir is consciously borrowing the die-having-mattered frame from a public-figure source. Same flavor as the "victory for humanity" impulse that recurs in the 2022 seven motivations list half a year later.
  • The pivot from grand-quote to "fix glasses at Walmart" is signature manifesto style note register — refuses to separate the cosmic from the cleanup-list.
  • "Eat with David" — recurring-friendship-maintenance pattern, same Dave who shows up across 2021 07 29 my guy dave and the 2025 todos.

Source quote

"Be ashamed to die, until you have scored some victory for humanity"

"2022 outline / Get fafsa taken care of / Get glasses fixed at Walmart / Call chiropractor / Emilio question review / Call ayoli for physical / Eat with David"

Open questions

  • Did the 2022 outline ever get written down? If so, where in the corpus?
  • Source of the Horace Mann quote — was Yahir reading something specific (a leadership book, John Maxwell, Dale Carnegie) at end of 2021?
  • "Ayoli" — physician name, single mention; track for re-appearance.

Wikilinks
